commit.message:
security: add RPC method allowlist to /api/rpc proxy

The /api/rpc endpoint was an unrestricted proxy forwarding any JSON-RPC
method to the backend node. Now only safe read-only methods and
eth_sendRawTransaction are allowed. All other methods return 403.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add End-Persistent signal and persistent session cleanup to HttpServer

Add support for explicit persistent session termination via the
"Qorus-Connection: End-Persistent" header in both HTTP/1.x and HTTP/2
persistent handler loops. On HTTP/2, disconnect() doesn't close the TCP
connection (multiplexed), so the server's persistent handler loop never
gets a close signal - the End-Persistent mechanism allows clients to
explicitly terminate persistent sessions.

Changes:
- handleMultiplexedPersistentSync: accept deregister_fn and sock_key
  params; handle End-Persistent signal; deregister persistent queue
  before returning to prevent race conditions; keep persistent handlers
  alive on idle timeout (only exit for non-persistent handlers)
- handlePersistentSync (HTTP/1.x): handle End-Persistent signal
- handleRequest catch block: clean up leaked persistent state when
  handler became persistent during a failed request but phi.handler
  was never set
- Add drainOrphanedPersistentRequests() to process requests pushed to
  the persistent queue during the race window between the last pq.get()
  and deregistration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Let agent generate missions instead of regex parser

processBrief() now saves raw brief only — no parsing, no template
mission generation. The session-start hook's firstSession() flow
handles it: injects the brief into agent context and tells the
agent to read it and generate project-specific missions.

Fixes: intent misdetection (ops vs build), wrong project names,
missed features from markdown-formatted briefs.

commit.message:
fix(mobile): release native AudioRecord on mute via ADM stopLocalRecording (BUG-001)

The previous fix (setAndroidAudioConfiguration) only changed the AudioManager
mode but didn't release the native AudioRecord inside JavaAudioDeviceModule.
The AudioRecord persists for the PeerConnection lifetime regardless of track
state, blocking Android's speech recognizer from accessing the mic.

Fix: call flutter_webrtc NativeAudioManagement.stopLocalRecording() on mute
(which calls audioDeviceModule.requestStopRecording() natively) to actually
release the AudioRecord. On unmute, startLocalRecording() prewarms the ADM
before restarting the track.

commit.message:
fix(mobile): toggle Android AudioManager mode on mute to release mic (BUG-001)

LiveKit SDK sets MODE_IN_COMMUNICATION on room connect and only clears
on disconnect. Muting stops the audio track but doesn't change the mode,
blocking Android's speech recognizer (keyboard STT).

Fix: call flutter_webrtc Helper.setAndroidAudioConfiguration() to switch
between communication and media modes on mute/unmute. Also reset mode
after reconnect if user is muted.

commit.message:
fix: use hand-written CJS preload to avoid ESM build issues

vite-plugin-electron generates ESM import/export in preload output
even with format:'cjs', breaking Electron's sandbox. Replaced with
a hand-written pure CJS preload file tracked in git, and removed
the preload entry from vite.config.ts.

commit.message:
fix: detect Acceptance Criteria custom field by display name via expand=names

Jira returns custom fields under their ID (customfield_14200), not their
display name, so key-pattern matching never found them. Now requests
expand=names on get_issue to get a field ID→display name map, then
reverse-lookups "Acceptance Criteria" (case/separator insensitive) to
find the correct field ID dynamically — no hardcoding required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

banner prompting the user to upload the EPUB to enable analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore finetune_multiseed to paper Section 7.5 exact hyperparameters

From paper Table 6 (the settings that gave Test R²=0.926±0.005, MAE=4.67yr):
  learning_rate:          1e-3 -> 5e-4
  batch_size x accum:     16x2=32 -> 32x2=64 (effective)
  dropout:                0.1  -> 0.2
  unfreeze_encoder_epoch: 3    -> 5
  encoder_lr:             1e-4 -> 5e-5 (10x lower via default 0.1 multiplier)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix article body not saving from Next.js editor by adding Markdown↔Portable Text conversion

The edit API route checked for `data.body` but ArticleEditor sent `bodyMarkdown`,
causing body edits to silently fail. Added bidirectional conversion:
- GET: Portable Text → Markdown (for editor textarea display)
- POST: Markdown → Portable Text (for Sanity storage)
Also fixed ArticleEditor to avoid [object Object] display when body is a PT array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: BTCLend - Bitcoin L1 Lending Protocol

Full-stack Aave-style lending protocol on Bitcoin L1 using OP_NET:
- LendingPool smart contract (AssemblyScript → WASM) with deposit, withdraw, borrow, repay, liquidate
- Next.js 16 frontend with dark DeFi theme (white/orange/brown)
- 6 lending markets (BTC live + 5 upcoming)
- OP Wallet integration with UnisatSigner
- Dashboard with positions, health factor, borrow limits
- Netlify deployment ready
